prior_block_findings_resolved.title: PlatformBadge orphan — no parent surface mounts it
prior_block_findings_resolved.resolution: RESOLVED. ClipCard.tsx line 441 mounts PlatformBadge at absolute bottom-left gated on `clip.platforms && clip.platforms.length > 0`. ClipWindow.tsx line 184 mounts PlatformBadge at absolute top-right (z-10, pointer-events-none) on the same gate. Both gates correctly hide the badge in the default empty-array / undefined state.

prior_block_findings_resolved.title: PlatformBadge has no data producer — types without Python writers
prior_block_findings_resolved.resolution: RESOLVED. sidecar.py adds method_set_clip_platforms (validates the 6 PlatformIds — youtube/tiktok/instagram/x/linkedin/facebook — with no escape hatch; rejects unknown ids via ValueError). Registered in METHODS dict at sidecar.py:2795. project.py create_imported_pack now writes `platforms: []` + `overlay_template: None` defaults on every imported clip (project.py:527-528). sidecar.ts adds applyOverlayTemplate + setClipPlatforms wrappers with signatures matching the Python params exactly.

prior_block_findings_resolved.title: OverlayTemplateGallery / OverlayTemplateKey union has no Python handler
prior_block_findings_resolved.resolution: RESOLVED at the data layer. sidecar.py adds method_apply_overlay_template (sidecar.py:1227-1273) with the canonical _OVERLAY_TEMPLATE_PRESETS dict mapping all 8 OverlayTemplateKey values to layout+position. None-template clear path handled (sidecar.py:1255-1258 — persists overlay_template = None and returns project). Unknown templates raise ValueError. The real-bake path routes through stages.apply_overlay_to_clip (verified to exist at stages.py:2015) when source_path is provided. Mount of OverlayTemplateGallery itself is explicitly deferred to v0.7.15 per Claude's note — data layer ready, UI mount pending. Acceptable per the defer-list pre-approval.

prior_block_findings_resolved.title: CANVAS_GRID_ROWS too small for Kimi's 20-tile cap
prior_block_findings_resolved.resolution: RESOLVED. workbench/types.ts raises CANVAS_GRID_ROWS 8 → 10. Comment notes 8 cols × 10 rows = 80 cells, fitting 20 × 2×2 tiles cleanly. Verified findFreeSlot loop bounds (useWorkbenchStore.ts:84-85) use the symbol, not a magic number — auto-adjusts. clampPos (useWorkbenchStore.ts:64-65) uses CANVAS_GRID_ROWS - size.h — correctly enforces new bound. WindowManager.tsx:336 sets gridTemplateRows: repeat(CANVAS_GRID_ROWS, ...) — visual grid matches logical bound. Persisted session restore path clamps incoming pos through clampPos so older 8-row layouts stay valid; no migration needed.

prior_block_findings_resolved.title: PlatformBadgePicker has no real data path — Kimi's component was decorative
prior_block_findings_resolved.resolution: RESOLVED. ClipPreview.tsx:987 mounts PlatformBadgePicker. onToggle (lines 989-998) computes the next array (toggle semantics), awaits sidecar.setClipPlatforms(slug, index, next), threads the new project via onProjectChange(r.project), and catches errors via setActionError(humanError(e)) — uses the canonical humanError pipeline, no raw String(e). The picker is reachable both via ResultsGrid (clip click → ClipPreview modal) and ClipEditDrawer (workbench tile → edit → ClipPreview).

new_findings.title: Silent strand — cut-from-source clips never get platforms / overlay_template defaults written
new_findings.severity: P2 (soft strand, not a hard bug — UI gates defend correctly)
new_findings.claim_implicit: Defaults persist on every clip via the imported-pack pathway.
new_findings.actual: llm.py:503-513 builds the cut-from-source clip dict (out_clips.append({...})) with NO platforms / overlay_template fields. The dict is persisted via project.set_clips() unchanged. So `clip.platforms` is undefined (not empty array) for cut-from-source clips. The TS type marks both optional, so this does not crash, AND the ClipCard + ClipWindow gates use `clip.platforms && clip.platforms.length > 0` which correctly evaluates falsy for undefined. PlatformBadgePicker uses `clip.platforms ?? []` so the picker still works. Net effect: end-user can still pick platforms on cut-from-source clips via the picker, but the underlying record shape is inconsistent between import and cut paths.
new_findings.evidence: llm.py:503-513 (cut-from-source clip factory), project.py:520-528 (import-pack defaults — note import is the ONLY path that writes the defaults), ClipCard.tsx:441 + ClipWindow.tsx:184 (truthy-array gates), ClipPreview.tsx:988 (?? [] coalescing)
new_findings.fix_required: Add `"platforms": []` + `"overlay_template": None` to the out_clips.append() dict in llm.py:503 — same 2-line surgical fix that landed on project.py:527-528. Keeps record shape consistent across both factory paths. No UI changes needed.
new_findings.defer_rationale: UI defends correctly via ?? [] coalescing and truthy-length gates — soft-strand only. Promote to P0 the moment any downstream code (publish routing, analytics aggregation, drip planner) starts iterating clip.platforms WITHOUT the nullish-coalescing guard.

new_findings.title: PlatformBadge TikTok glyph still renders Latin 'T' — pre-existing design regression carried into v0.7.14
new_findings.severity: P2 (cosmetic, noted in prior cycle as P2)
new_findings.claim_implicit: PlatformBadge ships with brand-correct icons for all 6 platforms.
new_findings.actual: PlatformBadge.tsx:11 + 48-49 + 102-103: TikTok has no Lucide icon, so the badge renders a literal Latin 'T' in a teal circle. Once founders see this they will read it as a placeholder glyph rather than the TikTok wordmark or musical-note. Carried over from v0.7.13 untouched.
new_findings.fix_required: Swap the 'T' fallback for an inline SVG of TikTok's monogram (the music-note glyph is 6 lines of SVG). Picker side has the same issue at line 103.
new_findings.defer_rationale: Cosmetic. Brand polish, not data correctness.

verdict_rationale: All 5 prior P0/P1 BLOCK findings are RESOLVED with honest data-producer + UI-mount fixes that match the brief verbatim. method_apply_overlay_template + method_set_clip_platforms register, validate strictly, and handle the clear/None paths. ClipCard + ClipWindow render PlatformBadge with correct truthy gates. ClipPreview wires PlatformBadgePicker through sidecar.setClipPlatforms with proper humanError catch + onProjectChange threading. CANVAS_GRID_ROWS raise propagates symbolically through findFreeSlot + clampPos + WindowManager grid template. All mechanical gates (tsc/py_compile/smoke-embed/humanError) green. The two new findings (P2-1 cut-path silent-strand, P2-2 TikTok glyph) are soft — UI defends correctly with ?? [] coalescing and truthy-length gates, and the deferred K4-K7 + OverlayTemplateGallery items are pre-approved as the v0.7.15 punch-list. Installed app at /Applications/Liquid Clips.app reports 0.7.14 — ship signal aligned with working tree.
v0_7_15_punch_list: P2-1 — Add `platforms: []` + `overlay_template: None` to llm.py:503 out_clips.append() so cut-from-source path matches import-pack record shape
P2-2 — Replace 'T' Latin letter fallback for TikTok in PlatformBadge.tsx:49 + :103 with inline SVG monogram
K4 — Mount LibraryQuickPreview into LibraryTab modal-on-tile-click
K5 — Native port BountySwipe into Earn tab (or accept webview Earn stays canonical)
K6 — Wire StudioTour with real [data-tour] anchors, replace OnboardingOverlay
K7 — Mount ActivityOrbit into WorkstationRoom avatar slot
OverlayTemplateGallery — UI mount + wire onSelect → sidecar.applyOverlayTemplate (data layer already ready)
F5 — sidecar auto-restart (60-90min Rust + TS, 1-retry cap)
ship_recommendation: Ready to commit v0.7.14 and push pending Daniel's explicit approval per the No-push-until-confirmed rule. The Daniel-review build at /Applications/Liquid Clips.app is already at 0.7.14 ready for the live walk.
